name: Korea Blockchain Week 2025 (KBW2025: IMPACT)
edition: 2025 edition; flagship conference branded "KBW2025: IMPACT". KBW is an annual event organized by FACTBLOCK since 2018.
location: Seoul, South Korea. Festival week spread across the city; flagship IMPACT conference held at Walkerhill Hotel & Resort (Vista Walkerhill / Grand Walkerhill), Gwangjin-gu, Seoul.
dates: Festival week: September 22-28, 2025. Flagship IMPACT conference: September 23-24, 2025.
attendance: Approximately 12,000-13,000+ attendees and ~300 speakers across the IMPACT conference, with 80+ official side events across Seoul (some promotional sources cited up to 17,000 for the broader week). For comparison, KBW2024 reported ~17,000 attendees at the main conference. Figures vary by source.
defi_themes: Stablecoins as core crypto infrastructure (settlement, payments, liquidity, cross-border remittance)
KRW-denominated (Korean won) stablecoins and the evolving Korean/US regulatory frameworks (GENIUS Act-era US policy, FSC engagement)
Real-world asset (RWA) tokenization and RWAfi (real-world-asset finance) - RWAs as programmable DeFi collateral, liquidity-pool assets, and media of exchange
Digital Asset Treasuries (DATs) / on-chain treasuries reshaping market infrastructure and corporate balance sheets
Institutional DeFi adoption and the convergence of TradFi with decentralized systems
Regulation and compliance as enablers of institutional on-chain finance (Washington policymakers + Korea FSC participation)
AI x DeFi convergence (verifiable compute, ZK proofs for on-chain data, agentic finance)
Perps / high-performance on-chain trading venues (Hyperliquid presence)

notable_panels.title: Stable Summit (stablecoin-focused forum at/around KBW2025)
notable_panels.topic: Dollar-pegged and fiat-pegged stablecoins, financial innovation, and regulatory design; stablecoins as settlement and payments infrastructure
notable_panels.speakers: Policymakers and stablecoin/issuer leaders (lineup not fully itemized in verifiable sources)

notable_panels.title: Korea RWA & Stablecoin Summit
notable_panels.topic: Real-world assets and stablecoins in Web3; brought together 20+ financial institutions and Web3 leaders on RWA/stablecoin adoption mechanics and infrastructure scaling
notable_panels.speakers: 20+ financial institutions and Web3 leaders (individual names not reliably itemized)

notable_panels.title: Asset Tokenization's Rising Tide
notable_panels.topic: Institutional implications of tokenized real-world assets (held ~Sept 24)

notable_panels.title: RWAfi / RWAiFi Summit (Robotics & AI Economy)
notable_panels.topic: Real-world asset finance, including tokenization of robotics/AI compute economy assets (GAIB AI's RWAiFi programming)

notable_panels.title: Verifiable: AI x DeFi Networking Event (official side event)
notable_panels.topic: Intersection of verifiable AI compute and DeFi across APAC/Cosmos ecosystems
notable_panels.speakers: Hosted by Nexus with Cosmostation; partners included Story Protocol, Osmosis, Babylon, Allora

notable_speakers.name: Arthur Hayes
notable_speakers.affiliation: CIO, Maelstrom (co-founder, BitMEX) - keynote on monetary policy / economic volatility

notable_speakers.name: Bo Hines
notable_speakers.affiliation: Former Executive Director, White House Council of Advisers on Digital Assets (later Tether USA) - US digital-asset policy

notable_speakers.name: Donald Trump Jr.
notable_speakers.affiliation: Co-Founder, American Bitcoin / World Liberty Financial association

key_takeaways: Stablecoins were the dominant DeFi narrative: framed as foundational crypto infrastructure anchoring liquidity, trading, payments, and emerging-market adoption, with multiple dedicated summits (Stable Summit, Asia Stablecoin Conference, Korea RWA & Stablecoin Summit).

KRW (Korean won) stablecoins were a focal regulatory theme - if approved, seen as powering merchant payments, loyalty programs, and digital remittances; Korea's FSC and US policymakers both engaged on frameworks.

RWAs are maturing from simple wrappers into programmable DeFi building blocks - used as collateral, in liquidity pools, and as media of exchange (RWAfi); strong institutional and Korean-market interest in an Asia RWA hub.
Digital Asset Treasuries (DATs) / on-chain treasuries emerged as a structural theme reshaping market infrastructure, corporate balance sheets, and compliance.

Institutional adoption and the TradFi-DeFi convergence were the overarching message, reinforced by unprecedented participation from Washington policymakers (White House, CFTC) and Korea's FSC bridging US and Asian regulatory frameworks.
AI x DeFi convergence (verifiable compute, ZK proofs like Brevis for scalable on-chain data, agentic finance) was a recurring cross-cutting theme.
USD1 (World Liberty Financial) was a notable stablecoin standout, emphasizing real-world payment integration (e.g., card/payment partnerships).
